summary: Bug fix verified: cover_url is now stored on posts and publish uses telegram_api.send_photo for large previews. Added new pytest class TestCoverUrl (5 tests) in /app/backend/tests/test_api.py — all pass. Full regression: 44/44 pytest tests pass (39 previous + 5 new). Verified via API: (1) POST /api/posts with is_cover=true photo → response.cover_url == that photo url, persisted on GET; (2) POST with only text blocks → cover_url is null (key present, value None); (3) POST with photos but no is_cover flag → cover_url falls back to FIRST photo url; (4) POST /api/posts/{id}/publish without configured channel returns HTTP 400 with Russian 'канал' detail (graceful, no 500), and post remains published=false; (5) Code-level: telegram_api.send_photo exists with signature (chat_id, photo_url, caption), server.publish_post source contains 'send_photo', 'send_message' and 'cover_url' — confirms send_photo is called when cover_url present, send_message is the fallback. Regression checked: auth login/me, auth gating on protected endpoints, /api/upload with watermark toggle (applies+converts to JPEG when enabled, untouched when disabled), watermark GET/PUT/preview endpoints, /api/media/{id} public serving, telegraph article rendering, cover ordering (is_cover block promoted to first in Telegraph output), stats schema, delete cascade. NOTE: as documented in the review request, actual Telegram sendPhoto call is NOT exercised (preview env has EMPTY TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN and no channel) — only cover_url storage, publish error handling, and code correctness verified. Cleanup: all TEST_ posts deleted (verified 0 remaining), watermark reset to disabled.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py publish_post: try/except fallback from send_photo to send_message swallows the send_photo exception silently (no logger). If Telegram rejects the downloaded image (e.g. file too large, wrong content-type, timeout) the user will get a sendMessage (small preview) with NO warning — which is exactly the bug they were fixing. Recommend logger.warning('sendPhoto failed, falling back to sendMessage: %s', e) inside the inner except.

telegram_api.send_photo downloads the image via httpx with a browser UA but does NOT verify content-type or size before forwarding to Telegram. Telegram's sendPhoto limit is 10 MB and must be image/jpeg|png|webp. If cover_url points to a huge file or an HTML page, the multipart POST will fail — currently surfaces as a generic ValueError. Consider: check img.headers['content-type'].startswith('image/') and len(img.content) < 10*1024*1024 before uploading, else raise a clear error (or fall back gracefully).

server.py create_post: cover_url currently only accepts PHOTO blocks (video is excluded from the fallback). If a post has only a video with is_cover=true, cover_url is None and publish will use send_message (small preview). Intentional? send_photo cannot handle videos anyway; document this or add a comment.

server.py: cover_url is added as a NEW field to the post document but existing posts (pre-fix) will have no 'cover_url' key. get_post/get_posts return them without the key; frontend must handle missing cover_url. Non-blocking (post.get('cover_url') is used in publish_post) but worth a migration or default projection.
Still using deprecated @app.on_event('startup') — carried over from previous iterations.

telegram_api.py: httpx client per-call (no connection pooling). Fine for low volume but each publish makes 2 outbound requests (download cover + upload to Telegram) with separate TLS handshakes. Consider a module-level AsyncClient if volume grows.
seed_data_creation: All test posts created inside test classes are self-cleaning via try/finally client.delete. Final state: 0 TEST_ posts, watermark disabled, no channel configured. Uploads inside TestCoverUrl.test_publish_without_channel_graceful_400 create GridFS media that IS cleaned up via post delete (media_ids cascade). Other uploads (via _upload helper in success tests) leave orphan GridFS files — same pattern as previous iterations, not an issue.
context_for_next_testing_agent: New pytest class: TestCoverUrl in /app/backend/tests/test_api.py — 5 tests covering the send_photo bug fix. Post document now has 'cover_url' field: null if no photos, else the URL of the is_cover=true photo, else the first photo URL. server.publish_post branches on cover_url: if present → telegram_api.send_photo (downloads image server-side with browser UA then multipart uploads as real photo to Telegram); else → send_message (link preview). Both wrapped in try/except → HTTP 400 with detail. Real Telegram calls CANNOT be tested in preview (empty TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN). To test large-preview behavior end-to-end, need a real bot token + channel on user's VPS. Code inspection (inspect.getsource on server.publish_post) is the current substitute — see test_telegram_api_has_send_photo_and_is_used.
rca_of_the_issue: Original bug: Telegram's link_preview_options.prefer_large_media does NOT apply to telegra.ph domain (Telegram-side rendering ignores it for their own service, giving a small square thumbnail). Fix: server.publish_post now downloads the cover image server-side (telegram_api.send_photo with browser UA to bypass hotlink checks) and uploads it as a real photo to Telegram's sendPhoto endpoint (multipart form), with title + telegraph link in the caption — Telegram then renders it as a large image. Sanity check + graceful fallback to send_message via try/except keeps posting resilient. cover_url selection: (1) explicit is_cover photo block wins, (2) else first photo block, (3) else null (send_message).
